visa_types.overview:
Overview
 Airport Transit visa allows you to wait for a connecting flight in the international area of the airport. This visa does not grant access to the Schengen area.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
 Airport Transit visa allows you to wait for a connecting flight in the international area of the airport. This visa does not grant access to the Schengen area.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Airport Transit (A)]
Methods of payment for visa fees:
- If the applicant submits their visa application at the Malta Visa Application Centre:
- Cash payment at the Malta Visa Application Centre on the day of appointment

[Table:  Airport Transit (A)]
Please Note:
- Special note for all applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre:
- The applicable visa fees in MAD depend on the current exchange rate, which can be changed without any notifications
- The visa fees need to be paid in cash at the Visa Application Centre
- Applicants who are exempted from the visa fees need to pay only the service fee
- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable

## Documents Required
Please see the checklist for an airport transit visa with the complete list of requirements. (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/malta/morocco/english/pdf/Transit-visa-Malta.pdf) 
- Complete the Schengen visa application form (https://foreign.gov.mt/schengen-visa-applications/),  print it out and take it to your appointment.
- The portal will guide through the application questions and will make sure you do not forget to enter any necessary values.

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, colour photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
- The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly. .
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in colour and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
- Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
For Schengen processing time will be a minimum of 15 working days and for D-National Malta Visa will be a minimum of 5 working days. However, for certain cases, the Consular Services may decide to extend the processing time

## Download Forms
- This page hosts the form required for your Visa Application. Please click on the relevant form to download your visa application form.
Visa Application Form  (https://foreign.gov.mt/schengen-visa-applications/)
- To Download a Form:
- Click on the formlink 
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
- OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target Asfrom the list 
- Savethe form on your computer.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you are traveling to Malta to visit family / friends (including girlfriend/boyfriend), you may apply for this visa.

visa_types.name: Short stay Visa
visa_types.overview:
If you wish to visit Malta for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Malta.
Short Stay visas allow the holder to transit through or remain in Malta territory and all other Schengen States for a maximum period of three months (90 days) within a period of six months (180 days) from the entry date into the Schengen Area. Nationals from countries included in the list of countries with the obligation to carry a visa in order to cross external borders must be in possession of this visa (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/malta/algeria/english/pdf/ListadopasessometidosvisadoEN.pdf). Please click the appropriate visa category for more information.
visa_types.full_text:
If you wish to visit Malta for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Malta.
Short Stay visas allow the holder to transit through or remain in Malta territory and all other Schengen States for a maximum period of three months (90 days) within a period of six months (180 days) from the entry date into the Schengen Area. Nationals from countries included in the list of countries with the obligation to carry a visa in order to cross external borders must be in possession of this visa (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/malta/algeria/english/pdf/ListadopasessometidosvisadoEN.pdf). Please click the appropriate visa category for more information.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing, cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities. If during any portion of your visit to Malta you intend to engage in business, or official duties you must refer to other relevant visa categories.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing, cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities. If during any portion of your visit to Malta you intend to engage in business, or official duties you must refer to other relevant visa categories.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Tourist Visa]
Methods of payment for visa fees:
- If the applicant submits their visa application at the Malta Visa Application Centre:
- Cash payment at the Malta Visa Application Centre on the day of appointment

[Table:  Tourist Visa]
Please Note:
- Special note for all applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre:
- The applicable visa fees in MAD depend on the current exchange rate, which can be changed without any notifications
- The visa fees need to be paid in cash at the Visa Application Centre
- Applicants who are exempted from the visa fees need to pay only the service fee
- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable

## Documents Required
Please see the checklist for a Tourism visa with the complete list of requirements. (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/malta/morocco/english/pdf/EN-Visa-Tourism-2024.pdf) 
- Complete the Schengen visa application form (https://foreign.gov.mt/schengen-visa-applications/), print it out and take it to your appointment.
- The portal will guide through the application questions and will make sure you do not forget to enter any necessary values. 

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, colour photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
- The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly. .
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in colour and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
- Note :Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
For Schengen processing time will be a minimum of 15 working days and for D-National Malta Visa will be a minimum of 5 working days. However, for certain cases, the Consular Services may decide to extend the processing time

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A visa for cultural, sports, religious, educational, research or vocational training purposes is required if during any portion of your stay you intend to:
- attend one or more cultural events;
- participate in one or more sports events;
- engage in religious activity;
- take part in educational activity;
- take part in research activity;
- take part in vocational training.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Sports and Cultural Visa]
Methods of payment for visa fees:
- If the applicant submits their visa application at the Malta Visa Application Centre:
- Cash payment at the Malta Visa Application Centre on the day of appointment

[Table:  Sports and Cultural Visa]
Please Note:
- Special note for all applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre:
- The applicable visa fees in MAD depend on the current exchange rate, which can be changed without any notifications
- The visa fees need to be paid in cash at the Visa Application Centre
- Applicants who are exempted from the visa fees need to pay only the service fee
- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable

## Documents Required
Please see the checklist for a Sports and Cultural Visa visa with the complete list of requirements. (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/malta/morocco/english/pdf/EN-Visa-Culture-Sports-2024.pdf) 
- Complete the Schengen visa application form (https://foreign.gov.mt/schengen-visa-applications/), print it out and take it to your appointment.
- The portal will guide through the application questions and will make sure you do not forget to enter any necessary values.

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, colour photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
- The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly. .
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in colour and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
- Note :Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
For Schengen processing time will be a minimum of 15 working days and for D-National Malta Visa will be a minimum of 5 working days. However, for certain cases, the Consular Services may decide to extend the processing time

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A business visa is required if during any portion of your visit you intend to conduct business or commercial activities, e.g. meetings, conferences, training, trade fairs.
